title: Caching
content:
### The Cache Framework Django's cache framework allows you to save expensive computations (database queries, API calls, rendered templates) so they don't have to be recomputed on every request. This dramatically improves performance. ### Cache Backends Configure in `settings.py`: **Local memory (development):** ```python CACHES = { 'default': { 'BACKEND': 'django.core.cache.backends.locmem.LocMemCache', 'LOCATION': 'unique-snowflake', } } ``` **Database cache:** ```bash python manage.py createcachetable ``` ```python CACHES = { 'default': { 'BACKEND': 'django.core.cache.backends.db.DatabaseCache', 'LOCATION': 'my_cache_table', } } ``` **Redis:** ```bash pip install django-redis ``` ```python CACHES = { 'default': { 'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache', 'LOCATION': 'redis://127.0.0.1:6379/1', 'OPTIONS': { 'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient', } } } ``` **Memcached:** ```python CACHES = { 'default': { 'BACKEND': 'django.core.cache.backends.memcached.PyMemcacheCache', 'LOCATION': '127.0.0.1:11211', } } ``` ### Per-View Cache Cache the entire output of a view: ```python from django.views.decorators.cache import cache_page @cache_page(60 * 15) # Cache for 15 minutes def article_list(request): # Expensive database query articles = Article.objects.filter(is_published=True).select_related('author') return render(request, 'articles/list.html', {'articles': articles}) ``` In URL conf: ```python from django.views.decorators.cache import cache_page urlpatterns = [ path('articles/', cache_page(60 * 30)(ArticleListView.as_view())), ] ``` ### Template Fragment Cache Cache specific parts of a template: ```html {% load cache %} {% cache 600 sidebar request.user.id %}
{% for recent_post in recent_posts %} {{ recent_post.title }} {% endfor %}
{% endcache %} ``` The format: `{% cache timeout key [key2 ...] %}{% endcache %}` ### Low-Level Cache API ```python from django.core.cache import cache # Set a value cache.set('my_key', 'my_value', timeout=300) # 5 minutes cache.set('user_data', {'name': 'Alice', 'role': 'admin'}, None) # never expires # Get a value value = cache.get('my_key') if value is None: # Cache miss — compute and store value = expensive_computation() cache.set('my_key', value, 300) # Get with default value = cache.get('missing_key', 'default_value') # Atomic get-or-set (Python 3.12+) value = cache.get_or_set('my_key', expensive_computation, timeout=300) # Delete cache.delete('my_key') # Check existence if cache.has_key('my_key'): print('Key exists') # Increment/Decrement cache.set('counter', 1) cache.incr('counter') # -> 2 cache.decr('counter') # -> 1 # Clear entire cache cache.clear() ``` ### Cache Timeout Timeouts are in seconds. Special values: - `None`: Cache never expires - `0`: Cache immediately expires (effectively disabled) - Default: 300 seconds (5 minutes), configured via `CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_SECONDS`: ```python CACHE_MIDDLEWARE_SECONDS = 600 # default timeout ``` ### Per-Site Cache Cache all pages (use carefully!): ```python MIDDLEWARE = [ 'django.middleware.cache.UpdateCacheMiddleware', # first # ... other middleware 'django.middleware.cache.FetchFromCacheMiddleware', # last ] ``` ### Cache Versioning ```python # Set with version cache.set('key', 'value', version=2) cache.get('key', version=2) # Increment all versions cache.incr_version('key') ``` ### Cache Key Prefixing ```python CACHES = { 'default': { 'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache', 'LOCATION': 'redis://127.0.0.1:6379/1', 'KEY_PREFIX': 'mysite', } } ``` This is useful when multiple sites share the same cache backend.

quiz.question: Which decorator is used to cache the entire output of a Django view?
How do you cache a specific section of a Django template?
